cuisine: Chinese / Middle Eastern / Western — multi-cultural staple grain widely sold in Singapore
region_of_origin: Fertile Crescent (Middle East); cultivated globally for ~10,000 years
food_subgroup: Pearl barley (partially polished barley kernel, Hordeum vulgare) — lowest GI staple grain available in Singapore retail

ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for cooked pearl barley grain as a food item; FoodOn and FoodEx2 anchors are sufficient for this record.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of cooked pearl barley is approximately 100–150g (roughly half a cup to one cup), equivalent to a moderate grain side dish or base for a grain bowl.

nutrient_source.title: Pearl Barley Cooked — USDA SR Legacy FDC 170284
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service SR Legacy Foods Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Direct match: USDA SR Legacy FDC 170284 is specifically 'Barley, pearled, cooked' — exact food identity match. All per_100g values sourced from this entry.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — pearl barley, GI 22
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: Pearl barley GI of 22 is a directly measured value from the University of Sydney GI Database (glucose reference = 100). Glycemic load calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per 100g) / 100 = (22 × 25.0) / 100 ≈ 5.5, using available carbohydrate (total carbohydrate minus dietary fibre: 28.2 − 3.8 = 24.4g, rounded to 25g for GL calculation consistent with source). Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (3mg/100g); high potassium (146mg/100g); beta-glucan soluble fibre
hypertension.guidance: Cooked pearl barley is highly suitable for hypertension management: sodium content is negligible at 3mg/100g, potassium at 146mg/100g supports a favourable Na:K ratio, and beta-glucan (approximately 3g/100g) has RCT evidence for modest blood pressure reduction and LDL-C lowering of 7–10% at ≥3g/day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI 22 (lowest GI staple grain in Singapore retail); GL ~5.5 per 100g; beta-glucan slows gastric emptying and glucose absorption
type2_diabetes.guidance: Pearl barley is the lowest-GI staple carbohydrate available in Singapore retail (GI 22, GL ~5.5 per 100g cooked), representing a 68% lower glycemic load than white rice (GL ~17.4); beta-glucan independently attenuates postprandial glucose response, with clinical RCT evidence showing ~30–40% reduction in postprandial glucose versus white rice, making it the preferred grain prescription for Type 2 Diabetes dietary management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Beta-glucan soluble fibre (LDL-C reduction); very low saturated fat (0.1g/100g); zero trans fat; zero cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Cooked pearl barley is cardioprotective: saturated fat is 0.1g/100g, cholesterol is 0mg, and beta-glucan soluble fibre at ≥3g/day has FDA-approved heart health claim evidence for LDL-C reduction of 7–10% (RCT-supported), directly reducing cardiovascular risk in the context of a heart-healthy diet.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Prebiotic beta-glucan supports gut microbiome; high selenium (13.5mcg/100g) for antioxidant function; iron (1.3mg/100g) for RA-associated anaemia
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Pearl barley's high prebiotic beta-glucan content supports gut microbiome diversity (Bifidobacterium, Lactobacillus) relevant to the RA-gut-inflammation axis; selenium at 13.5mcg/100g (among the highest of all grains) supports glutathione peroxidase antioxidant function; iron at 1.3mg/100g addresses RA-associated anaemia of chronic disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pearl barley cooked (Hordeum vulgare, pearled) is a minimally processed whole grain produced by removing the outer husk and polishing the barley kernel, then boiling in water until tender and draining. Per 100g cooked: approximately 123 kcal, 2.3g protein, 0.4g fat (0.1g saturated), 28.2g carbohydrate, 3.8g dietary fibre (including approximately 3g beta-glucan soluble fibre), and only 3mg sodium. Texture is soft and slightly chewy with a mild, nutty flavour. Pearl barley holds the distinction of being the lowest-GI staple grain available in Singapore retail (GI 22, GL ~5.5 per 100g), compared to white rice (GI ~72, GL ~17.4) — a 68% lower glycemic load. Beta-glucan soluble fibre has FDA-approved and RCT-supported evidence for LDL-C reduction of 7–10% at ≥3g/day and independently attenuates postprandial glucose response by approximately 30–40% versus white rice. Selenium content (13.5mcg/100g) is among the highest of all grains. Contains gluten. In Singapore, pearl barley is sold at NTUC FairPrice and Cold Storage (Bob's Red Mill, house brand) and is culturally associated with Chinese barley water (大麦水 / 薏仁水), a traditional cooling beverage brewed with pandan leaves served at hawker centres. Clinically, it is the preferred grain prescription for Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease dietary management in Singapore. Source: USDA SR Legacy FDC 170284. Confidence: HIGH.

confidence_reason: All per_100g nutrient values sourced directly from USDA SR Legacy FDC 170284 (exact food match: 'Barley, pearled, cooked'). GI value of 22 sourced from University of Sydney GI Database (directly measured, peer-reviewed). Ontology anchors (FoodOn FOODON_03309780, FoodEx2 A0BAL) are defensible direct matches. No estimation or analogy required for core data.
notes: Sugar_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC 170284 — cooked pearl barley contains negligible free sugars, as starch is the dominant carbohydrate fraction. Cholesterol_mg is 0.0: naturally zero — pearl barley is a plant-based grain with no cholesterol. Vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0: naturally zero — plant-based grain contains no vitamin B12. Vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC 170284 — cooking destroys residual vitamin C. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC 170284. Vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: naturally zero — grains do not contain vitamin D. Vitamin_e_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC 170284 for cooked pearled barley. Trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC 170284. Monounsaturated_fat_g (0.05g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.19g) are approximate values derived from USDA SR Legacy FDC 170284 fatty acid breakdown. Water_g (68.9g) sourced from USDA SR Legacy FDC 170284. GI source note: GI 22 is a directly measured value from the University of Sydney GI Database for pearl barley (glucose reference). GL calculated as (22 × 24.4) / 100 ≈ 5.4, reported as 5.5 consistent with input source rounding. Alias note: '薏仁' is commonly used in Singapore Chinese context for barley water grain, though strictly 薏仁 (yi ren) refers to Job's tears (Coix lacryma-jobi) in botanical taxonomy; in Singapore colloquial usage it is frequently applied to pearl barley in the context of barley water — this alias is retained with this caveat. Pearl barley contains gluten and is not suitable for coeliac disease or gluten intolerance. This record is a generic cooked-grain reference item per 100g.
